history.qwen_observation: The Spring Boot 3.3 upgrade recipe updated the parent to 3.3.13, which imports selenium-bom:4.2.2 that is missing from the local Nexus, causing a POM resolution failure during mvn compile.
history.qwen_rationale: Switching to UpgradeSpringBoot_3_2 targets Spring Boot 3.2.x, which is the minimum version supporting Java 21 per the compatibility matrix and avoids the missing selenium-bom:4.2.2 import present in 3.3.13. The rest of the chain remains unchanged to handle plugin upgrades, JDK bump, and Java 21 transforms.

history.qwen_observation: POM resolution fails because Spring Boot 3.3's dependency BOM imports selenium-bom:4.2.2, which is missing from the internal Nexus repository.
history.qwen_rationale: A POM_PATCH step with force_version on selenium-bom to 4.18.1 is inserted before the Spring Boot upgrade to override the unresolvable BOM version. This should allow Maven to resolve the model so that subsequent recipe steps and the Java 21 build can proceed.

history.qwen_rationale: Updating the selenium.version property to a modern, resolvable version (4.22.0) before applying the Spring Boot 3.3 upgrade ensures the BOM imports a valid artifact, allowing the build to succeed post-upgrade.

history.qwen_observation: Compilation failed at spring_boot_3_upgrade because mvcMatchers and WebSecurityConfigurerAdapter are removed in Spring Security 6, and the migration recipe did not fully resolve the API changes.
history.qwen_rationale: Re-running UpgradeSpringBoot_3_3 should apply the necessary Spring Security 6 migrations (mvcMatchers to requestMatchers, WebSecurityConfigurerAdapter to SecurityFilterChain), enabling successful compilation under JDK 17 before proceeding to JDK 21 plugin and build upgrades.
